exemplar.title: To what extent does Daphne Du Maurier explore the role of memory through the characters in Rebecca?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent does Daphne Du Maurier explore the role of memory through the characters in Rebecca?

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: The research question is clearly stated, tightly framed, and directly connected to the analysis of memory through three characters.
The topic is communicated accurately and consistently revisited in each section heading, providing a clear line of enquiry.
A complete table of contents and structured section headings demonstrate strong planning.
The methodology paragraph outlines both primary and secondary sources and the three focal effects, giving an effective roadmap.
weaknesses: Methodology lacks justification for source selection and relies on popular rather than scholarly sources.
Minor digressions into historical context occasionally interrupt the central focus.
Some repetition of purpose in the introduction prevents maximum clarity.
The title could more precisely specify the primary text under investigation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place topic communication and research-question focus firmly in the 5â6 band and methodology in the 3â4 band. The student communicates purpose and focus very effectively, with only brief tangents and some repetition. The research question is tightly framed and consistently connected to analysis. The methodology is serviceable but would benefit from a wider range of academic sources and justification for their use. On balance, this warrants a high-band score of 5 out of 6.

strengths: Demonstrates solid knowledge of Rebeccaâs plot, character dynamics, and Gothic conventions.
Effective integration of literary terminology such as âsymbolism,â âunreliable narrator,â and âfoil.â
Contextual understanding of 1930s social norms enriches analysis.
Quotations from secondary scholarship (Laing) are well woven into the text to support claims.
weaknesses: Occasional misapplication of terms (e.g., âthird-person characterisationâ) reduces precision.
Verb tense inconsistencies disrupt clarity when discussing narrative evolution.
Several interpretative assertions rely on speculative readings without rigorous scholarly support.
Secondary sources are listed without commentary on credibility or selection criteria.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student shows good knowledge of both primary text and critical context, with mostly accurate use of terminology. However, slips in terminology, uneven scholarly depth, and reliance on speculative interpretations keep this work in the 3â4 band. Strengths in contextual framing and integration of quotations support a solid mid-band score of 4 out of 6.

strengths: Arguments move beyond summary to nuanced interpretations of symbolism, power dynamics, and memory framing.
Textual quotations and secondary commentary are used to support most conclusions.
Counterpoints (e.g., narrator reliability) are acknowledged, demonstrating critical awareness.
Compelling insights into the narratorâs anonymity and Danversâ obsession show depth of thought.
weaknesses: Some arguments become repetitive and certain analytic leaps (e.g., sexuality readings) lack sufficient evidence.
Reliance on non-scholarly sources weakens overall research rigour.
Evaluation remains surface-level with few explicit reflections on methodological limitations.
The conclusion does not explicitly restate the research question against the final findings.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay exhibits good research quality and relevance, mostly supported analysis, and coherent argument development, aligning with a 7â9 band in some strands and 4â6 in others. However, thin evidence for certain claims, occasional tangents, and a lack of deeper methodological reflection moderate the performance. A mid-band score of 6 out of 12 appropriately reflects the âadequateâgoodâ level of critical thinking demonstrated.

strengths: Follows the required EE structure with title page, word count, table of contents, labeled sections, conclusion, and works cited.
Basic layout conventions (margins, headings, spacing) are correctly applied.
The works-cited list is well-formatted and includes detailed conference proceedings.
Transitions between major sections are generally clear, and core elements are present.
weaknesses: In-text citations are inconsistent (mix of page references and bare URLs).
The table of contents omits page numbers, hindering navigability.
Paragraph transitions can be abrupt, and long blocks of text lack sub-headings.
Unclear whether page numbering appears on each page.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Structure and formatting meet the core EE requirements, demonstrating a clearly appropriate organization. Minor lapses in in-text citation consistency, missing page numbers in the table of contents, and occasional abrupt transitions prevent a top-band rating. This performance aligns with the 2â3 band, so a score of 3 out of 4 is justified.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student outlines a basic method (close reading of the primary text supported by secondary sources) and lists sources in a works-cited section.  There is some evidence of informed selection: quotations are page-referenced, and at least one academic conference paper is used.  However, many sources are popular blogs or newspaper articles, and there is no explanation of how they were chosen or evaluated.  The range is serviceable rather than wide, indicating a mostly complete but not fully effective methodology.

Basic layout conventions (margins, headings, double-spacing) are met and an alphabetised works-cited list is supplied.  The student states the word count and provides a title.  However, in-text citations are inconsistent (some page numbers, some bare URLs), and it is unclear whether page numbering appears on each page.  These minor lapses reduce the score slightly but overall formatting remains competent.

The student gathers a reasonable body of evidence: multiple textual quotations, contextual remarks, and external commentary.  Most material clearly relates to memory and the three focal characters.  Nonetheless, some sources are tangential or lack scholarly rigour, and no primary archival research is attempted.  The research therefore sits comfortably in the âadequateâgoodâ range rather than âexcellent.â

The topic â the role of memory in Du Maurierâs Rebecca â is identified in the title, restated in the opening paragraph, and revisited in each section heading.  The essay never strays into unrelated areas and the reader can follow a clear line of enquiry.  Minor digressions into historical context (e.g., the Great Depression) occur, but they are brief and do not obscure the central focus.  Overall the purpose and focus are conveyed accurately, though occasional repetition prevents it from reaching the very top of the band.

A coherent argument is maintained, leading to a clear conclusion that revisits the research question.  The student does attempt evaluationâcontrasting Maximâs and Danversâ responses to memory and commenting on feminist/queer readingsâbut the critique remains surface-level and occasionally descriptive.  Logical progression is sound yet not consistently incisive, meriting a mid-band âadequate to goodâ score.

Paragraph-by-paragraph, the essay moves beyond narrative summary to interpret symbolism (pearls, shadow), power dynamics (Oedipal reading), and narrative structure (memory framing).  Claims are usually supported with textual quotation or secondary comment, and counter-points (e.g., reliability of the narrator) are acknowledged.  However, some lines of argument become repetitive, and a few leaps in logic (e.g., equating Danversâ grief with implied sexuality) are only thinly evidenced, placing the work at the lower end of the âgoodâ band.

The essay follows the required EE structure: title page with word count, table of contents, labelled sections, conclusion, and works cited.  Headings match the outline, and transitions between sections are clear.  All core elements (introduction, body, conclusion, references) are present, indicating a clearly appropriate structure.

The student shows solid knowledge of Rebecca, accurately recounting plot, character relationships, and key quotations.  Contextual understanding of Gothic conventions and 1930s social norms is evident, and secondary voices are woven into the discussion.  Nonetheless, the scholarship cited is limited in depth, and several assertions rely on speculative readings (e.g., Freudian lens) without rigorous scholarly backing, keeping the work at a âgoodâ rather than âexcellentâ level.

Literary terms such as âsymbolism,â âunreliable narrator,â âfemme fatale,â and âfoilâ are employed correctly and integrated smoothly into the prose.  Concepts like Gothic setting and Freudian dynamics are generally accurate, though sometimes broad.  Occasional slips (âthird-person characterisationâ used for what is really communal testimony) prevent fully consistent precision, but terminology use is mostly accurate and appropriate.

The research question is explicitly stated and tightly framed around "the extent" to which memory is explored through three named characters.  Each body section maps directly onto this focus, demonstrating a close connection between question and discussion.  Although the phrasing is slightly broad (âto what extentâ), the analysis consistently interprets this as an exploration of three specific effects (torment, powerlessness, obsession), showing a clear and purposeful direction.

annotations.comment: The word count of 3996 stays within the 4000-word limit, demonstrating attention to IB requirements.
The table of contents lists all major sections but omits page numbers, hindering navigability. Include page references for clarity.
The inclusion of a complete table of contents with all section headings demonstrates strong structural planning.
Table cell borders and spacing appear inconsistent, affecting readability. Apply consistent table formatting conventions.
The title is broad and does not specify the primary text under investigation. Consider including âRebeccaâ to sharpen the focus and clearly signal the novel analyzed.
The research question is clearly framed and directly ties to the investigation of memory through character analysis, providing strong directional focus.
The methodology paragraph clearly outlines primary and secondary sources and the three effects to be analyzed, providing an effective roadmap.
The methodology description lacks justification for why certain secondary sources were chosen over others. Add explanation of selection criteria.
Listing secondary sources generically without commenting on their credibility misses an opportunity to demonstrate informed selection.
Integrating Maximâs character analysis with direct quotations demonstrates effective textual engagement.
The symbolism of âpearlsâ and âsatinâ is identified but the essay does not define what constitutes symbolism. Include a clear definition and framework.
The introduction effectively establishes novel context (Great Depression, Gothic style) which situates the reader and demonstrates sound background knowledge.
The introduction outlines thematic elements but does not explain why the three memory effects were chosen. Clarify selection criteria to strengthen focus.
Reliance on a popular blog (Kennedy) without noting its scholarly status weakens source credibility. Critically evaluate and justify source selection.
Novel titles are inconsistently italicized; ensure âRebeccaâ and other works are formatted uniformly in italics.
Transitions between analytical points are abrupt, making the argument feel disjointed. Add linking sentences to improve flow.
Applying a Freudian lens enriches the analysis of power dynamics but should reference core psychoanalytic concepts for scholarly depth.
The discussion linking parental imagery to power imbalance provides a nuanced interpretation of narratorâMaxim relations.
The term âthird-person characterisationâ is misapplied here since the narrative remains first-person. Correct the terminology to maintain precision.
The leap from Maximâs confession of murder to broader memory symbolism is abrupt; elucidate the connection more explicitly.
Linking the Gothic setting of Manderley to themes of imprisonment is effectively argued and demonstrates insightful contextual understanding.
The citation for Felicity is presented as a bare URL without page references; ensure in-text citations conform to a single consistent style.
The opening of the âPowerless against the Pastâ section is impactful but needs a clearer transition marker from the previous section.
The interpretation of the narratorâs powerlessness is well-supported by quotations and secondary commentary on unreliable narration.
Verb tense shifts between past and present in this paragraph cause confusion. Review tense consistency when discussing narratorâs evolution.
The claim about the narratorâs unnamed status powerfully underscores her lack of identity; this insight is particularly compelling.
Long paragraphs without sub-headings can overwhelm the reader. Consider inserting minor headings or bullet points to improve readability.
Citations appear inconsistently formattedâsome include page numbers, others URLs. Adopt a uniform citation style throughout the text.
Characterizing Mrs Danversâ obsession through third-person narrative effectively illustrates memoryâs power to prolong presence beyond death.
Substantial assertions about queer undertones lack supporting evidence from queer theory; integrating such scholarship would fortify the claim.
Quotations from Laing are well integrated to support interpretative claims, enhancing scholarly depth.
The conclusion revisits core findings but omits explicit restatement of the research question; reframe key points against the original question.
Quotation from Du Maurierâs memoir enriches the conclusion by providing authorial insight into memoryâs role.
Block quotations in the conclusion are not indented as per style guidelines; apply consistent block quote formatting.
Including a brief evaluation of methodological strengths and limitations would enhance the reflective depth of the essay.
The Works Cited list includes scholarly conference proceedings correctly formatted with event details, demonstrating attention to citation conventions.
URLs in several citations lack access dates, which is essential for online sources. Add retrieval dates for accuracy.
The final listed entries maintain consistent formatting style, reflecting careful proofreading of the citation list.
The journal title in the Britannica citation is correctly italicized and includes a date, exemplifying strong source referencing.
